stories.title: Hammer Fire 100% Contained — What COS Families Should Know About Wildfire Preparedness

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Hammer Fire near Hanover: 100% contained. Evacuations lifted April 23. No structures lost.

Hanover School District 28 schools are back open.

A reminder to update your family's wildfire plan this spring. 🔥

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
The Hammer Fire is out — and COS families can breathe a little easier this morning.

The nearly 5,000-acre fire that broke out southeast of Colorado Springs on April 22 was declared 100% contained on April 23. Evacuation orders for the Hanover area were lifted that same day, and Hanover School District 28 — which closed all schools on April 23 due to fire activity — has since reopened.

No structures were lost. That's the good news.

But fire season in Colorado doesn't wait for summer. And this week's Hammer Fire is a reminder that families in Colorado Springs and El Paso County need wildfire plans — not just in the mountains, but in and around the city too.

Here's what you can do right now:

**Know your zone.** El Paso County's Wildfire Risk map is at elpasoco.com/emergency-management. Find out if your neighborhood is in a high-risk area.

**Build a go-bag.** For each family member, pack three days of water, medications, documents (birth certificates, insurance), phone chargers, and pet supplies. Keep it accessible.

**Talk with your kids.** Children handle emergencies better when they've heard the plan before. Walk through what to do if you need to leave quickly.

**Follow Watch Duty.** Download the Watch Duty app for real-time fire tracking in El Paso County.

The Colorado Springs Fire Department's Ready, Set, Go program has free guides at coloradosprings.gov. Spring and summer are unpredictable — it's never the wrong time to update your family's plan.

Are you prepared? What's one thing you've done to get your family wildfire-ready?

---

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: The Hammer Fire near Hanover is 100% contained. No structures lost. A reminder that wildfire season in Colorado starts early — does your family have a plan?

article.headline: Hammer Fire 100% Contained: What Colorado Springs Families Should Know About Wildfire Preparedness

research.facts:
Hammer Fire
**Source 1:** CBS Colorado — "Hammer Fire in rural area southeast of Colorado Springs forces evacuations, school closures"
- Fire started April 22, 2026 around 5 p.m. near Hammer Road and Hanover Road
- Burned nearly 5,000 acres (reports vary: 4,900 acres and ~4,200 acres cited)
- El Paso County Sheriff's Office issued evacuation orders for Hammer Road east to South Peyton Highway, Myers Road north to Squirrel Creek
- Hanover School District 28: all schools closed April 23 due to fire activity
- Emergency shelters: Hanover Jr/Sr High School (small animals/people), Norris Penrose Event Center (large animals)
**Confidence: HIGH** (multiple local news sources)
**Source 2:** KRDO — "'Hammer Fire' near Hanover reaches 100% containment"
- 100% contained; evacuation orders lifted April 23
- No structures lost
**Confidence: HIGH**
**Source 3:** Colorado Sun — "Evacuation order lifted for wildfire burning southeast of Colorado Springs"
- Confirms evacuation orders lifted April 23
**Confidence: HIGH**

stories.title: Colorado Cosmic Con Is This Weekend — Family Fun at Norris Penrose

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
TODAY + TOMORROW at Norris Penrose:

Colorado Cosmic Con
Sat April 25: 10 AM–6 PM
Sun April 26: 10 AM–5 PM
Admission: $12.51

100+ vendors, cosplay, celebrity guests. Tickets at coloradocosmiccon.com

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
COS parents — it's not too late to plan something fun for this weekend.

Colorado Cosmic Con is happening RIGHT NOW at the Norris Penrose Event Center (1045 Lower Gold Camp Road), and it runs through tomorrow too.

Here's the quick rundown for families:

📅 Saturday, April 25: 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
📅 Sunday, April 26: 10 a.m. to 5 p.m.
💵 Admission: $12.51 per person | Tickets: coloradocosmiccon.com

This year's theme is "the mafia era," with celebrity guests from The Sopranos and Goodfellas alongside voice actors from the One Piece anime. There are more than 100 vendors and artists, cosplay competitions, interactive panels, and photo opportunities with celebrities.

Best for: older kids, tweens, teens, and adults who love pop culture, comics, cosplay, or just a lively Saturday in Colorado Springs. Younger kids who are into superheroes will find plenty to enjoy too — it can just get loud and busy, so plan accordingly for little ones.

Over 3,500 people are expected across both days.

What are your plans this weekend, COS?

---

stories.title: New Pew Research — Most Teens Say Social Media Doesn't Hurt Their Mental Health (But 37% Say TikTok Hurts Their Sleep)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
New Pew Research: most teens say TikTok and Instagram don't hurt their mental health.

But 37% say TikTok hurts their sleep. Parents are more worried than teens about screen time.

Worth a conversation tonight.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Here's something that might surprise you: most teenagers say TikTok, Instagram, and Snapchat don't hurt their mental health.

That's one of the key findings from a major new Pew Research Center study released April 15, 2026. The researchers surveyed 1,458 teen-parent pairs and found that the majority of teens across all three platforms say the apps neither help nor hurt their mental health. Only 9%–11% of teen users reported that their platform had hurt their mental health.

But there's a significant wrinkle in the data: sleep.

37% of teen TikTok users say the app hurts how much sleep they get — compared to 26% for Snapchat and 24% for Instagram. And we all know what sleep-deprived kids look and feel like by Thursday morning.

Then there's the parent-teen gap. 44% of parents of teen TikTok users think their kid spends too much time on the app. Only 28% of teens agree. That's a pretty wide disagreement about what "too much" looks like.

The study's takeaway isn't that social media is harmless — it's that the relationship between platforms and wellbeing is more complicated than the headline "social media is bad for teens" suggests. Context matters. How long, when, and how a teen uses a platform shapes the impact.

For COS parents, the sleep finding is probably the most actionable. Charging devices outside the bedroom at night removes a lot of the temptation for late-night scrolling, without requiring a full household ban.

What rules do you have around your kid's phone at bedtime?

---

#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: New Pew Research: most teens say TikTok, Instagram, and Snapchat don't hurt their mental health — but 37% say TikTok hurts their sleep. Parents and teens have very different opinions on "too much" screen time.

article.headline: New Pew Research: Most Teens Say TikTok and Instagram Don't Hurt Their Mental Health — But Parents Disagree

research.facts:
Pew Research on Teens and Social Media
**Source 1:** Pew Research Center — "Teens' Experiences on TikTok, Instagram and Snapchat" (released April 15, 2026)
- Survey of 1,458 teen-parent dyads; conducted Sept. 25–Oct. 9, 2025
- Ages 13-17
- ~9 in 10+ teens use each platform for entertainment
- Mental health: most teens say platforms neither hurt nor help; only 9% (Snapchat/TikTok) and 11% (Instagram) say it hurt their mental health
- Sleep: 37% of TikTok users say it hurts their sleep; 26% Snapchat; 24% Instagram
- Parent-teen gap: 44% of parents of TikTok users think teen spends too much time; 28% of teens agree
**Confidence: HIGH** (direct from Pew Research, primary source)

stories.title: CPSC April 23 Recalls — Youth ATVs (1 Death Reported) + Children's Toothbrush Packaging (Battery Hazard)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
CPSC recalls — April 23:

⚠ Lil Pick Up Youth ATVs: one death reported, stop use now
⚠ Autobrush Sonic Pro Kids' Toothbrush: coin battery accessible, ingestion hazard

Both sold on Amazon. See cpsc.gov for details.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Two new CPSC recalls from April 23 — both involving products marketed to children.

**Lil Pick Up Youth All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs)**

The Consumer Product Safety Commission is recalling Lil Pick Up Youth ATVs due to crash and burn hazards. The vehicles violate mandatory ATV safety standards. One death has been reported in connection with these vehicles. If your family owns one, stop use immediately and contact the manufacturer for refund or remedy information. These were sold on Amazon.

**Autobrush Sonic Pro Children's Toothbrush Boxes**

If you've bought your kids the Autobrush Sonic Pro electric toothbrush, check the packaging. The CPSC recalled these boxes because children can easily access a lithium coin battery inside the packaging — posing a serious ingestion hazard. The packaging also lacks required warning labels under Reese's Law, which specifically regulates products containing button cell and coin batteries because of their danger to young children if swallowed.

Both products were sold on Amazon. As always, before buying any children's product on Amazon, we recommend checking cpsc.gov for recall status — the platform makes it easy to list recalled items for sale.

A reminder: the CPSC recall hotline is 1-800-638-2772. You can also sign up for email recall alerts at cpsc.gov/Recalls.

Which of these products have you seen or bought?

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: CPSC April 23 recalls: Lil Pick Up Youth ATVs (one death reported, stop use now) and Autobrush Sonic Pro Children's Toothbrush Boxes (coin battery ingestion risk). Both sold on Amazon.

article.headline: CPSC April 23 Recalls: Youth ATVs (One Death) and Children's Toothbrush Packaging (Battery Hazard)

research.facts:
CPSC April 23 Recalls
**Source 1:** CPSC.gov press release (via PR Newswire, April 23, 2026)
- "Lil Pick Up Youth All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs)" recalled — crash and burn hazards; violates mandatory ATV standard; one death reported
**Confidence: HIGH** (CPSC official source)
**Source 2:** CPSC.gov press release — "Autobrush Sonic Pro Children's Toothbrush Boxes"
- Recalled due to risk of injury or death from battery ingestion
- Lithium coin battery accessible by children
- Packaging does not have required Reese's Law warning labels
- Sold on Amazon
**Confidence: HIGH** (CPSC official source)
Note: Specific recall quantities, dates, prices, and model numbers for the Lil Pick Up ATVs and Autobrush were not confirmed from the available sources. Marking these as MEDIUM pending secondary source verification.
**Confidence (specific model details): MEDIUM** — use general product descriptions, avoid specific numbers not confirmed

stories.title: Colorado Preschool Case Heading to U.S. Supreme Court — What COS Families Should Know

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Supreme Court will hear Colorado's preschool case. Two Catholic schools were excluded from UPK for restricting LGBTQ family enrollment.

A ruling could change how COS families access free preschool.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Colorado parents with preschoolers: a case affecting the state's Universal Preschool program is heading to the U.S. Supreme Court.

Here's the background: In 2023, two Catholic preschools in Colorado sued the state after being excluded from the Universal Preschool (UPK) program. The state's UPK initiative offers families 15 free hours per week of preschool — a significant benefit for families with 4-year-olds. But the program requires participating providers to serve all families without discriminating based on LGBTQ status.

The Catholic preschools argued that being excluded from UPK because of their religious admission policies violates their First Amendment right to religious freedom. The Supreme Court agreed to hear the case, meaning a ruling is coming — though no timeline has been announced.

What does this mean for COS families?

If the Supreme Court rules in favor of the schools, it could open the door for more faith-based programs to join UPK — which would expand options for families who prefer religious settings. It could also potentially limit the anti-discrimination requirements for UPK providers.

If the Court rules against the schools, it affirms Colorado's current approach: UPK access for families comes with the requirement that providers serve all families equally.

Either way, the decision will reshape how Colorado families — including those in Colorado Springs — access and choose publicly funded preschool programs. There are multiple UPK sites in COS, and the ruling could affect enrollment choices for the 2027-28 school year and beyond.

We'll keep tracking this for COS families. What questions do you have about UPK in Colorado Springs?

---

#### Image Caption -- 5:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: The U.S. Supreme Court will hear Colorado's Catholic preschool vs. UPK case. A ruling could change how families in Colorado Springs access publicly funded preschool. We'll keep you updated.

article.headline: Colorado Preschool Case Goes to the Supreme Court: What COS Families With Preschoolers Need to Know

research.facts:
Colorado Preschool Supreme Court Case
**Source 1:** CPR News — "Colorado Catholic preschools' case over UPK headed to the Supreme Court" (April 20, 2026)
- Two Catholic preschools in Colorado sued the state in 2023
- Issue: Colorado's Universal Preschool (UPK) program excludes faith-based programs that discriminate based on LGBTQ family status in admission
- Supreme Court agreed to hear the case
- Schools argue: First Amendment religious freedom violation
- This is a First Amendment/religion and government case affecting preschool access statewide
**Confidence: HIGH** (CPR News, verified Colorado news outlet)
**Context:**
- Colorado Springs has multiple UPK provider sites
- COS families with preschoolers enrolled in UPK would be affected by any policy changes
- No decision date set yet
**Confidence: MEDIUM** (COS-specific claims are general, not from specific local source)
